2023.total_payment_impact: +$1.4 billion vs FY2022 (largest increase in 25 years)

provider_payment_factors.wage_index: Made permanent a 5% cap on any single-year decrease in a hospital's wage index; continued low-wage-index hospital policy; included urban-to-rural reclassified hospital wage data in the rural floor.
provider_payment_factors.dsh_uncompensated_care: Continued Medicare DSH and uncompensated care payment methodology; supplemental payment for Indian Health Service / Tribal and Puerto Rico hospitals.
provider_payment_factors.ntap: Continued/updated New Technology Add-on Payments for qualifying technologies.
provider_payment_factors.quality_programs: Established the 'Birthing-Friendly' hospital designation; advanced health-equity measures (RFI and new measures in Hospital IQR/VBP).

key_changes: +4.3% operating payment update for IQR + meaningful-EHR hospitals.
Permanent 5% cap on year-over-year wage index decreases.
Health-equity focused quality measures and reporting.

provider_payment_factors.wage_index: Continued the low-wage-index hospital policy and the 5% cap on wage index decreases.
provider_payment_factors.dsh_uncompensated_care: Updated Medicare DSH / uncompensated care payments.
provider_payment_factors.ntap: Continued NTAP for 11 technologies still considered new; discontinued 15 no longer new; net NTAP decrease of about $460 million.
provider_payment_factors.quality_programs: Finalized a health-equity adjustment to hospitals' Total Performance Score in the Hospital VBP Program (rewarding care for dual-eligible patients).

key_changes: Severity of the three homelessness ICD-10-CM codes (Z59.0-) changed from NonCC to CC.
Health-equity adjustment added to the Hospital VBP Total Performance Score.
15 new MS-DRGs created and 16 deleted (net 766 MS-DRGs).

2025.total_payment_impact: approximately +$3.2 billion vs FY2024 (DSH uncompensated care about -$0.2 billion)

provider_payment_factors.wage_index: Adopted the revised OMB Core-Based Statistical Area (CBSA) delineations (2023) for the wage index.
provider_payment_factors.dsh_uncompensated_care: Projected Medicare DSH uncompensated care payments to decrease by about $0.2 billion; MDH and low-volume hospital provisions set to expire Dec 31, 2024 (about -$0.4 billion if they expire).
provider_payment_factors.ntap: Increased the NTAP percentage from 65% to 75% for certain gene therapies used to treat sickle cell disease.
provider_payment_factors.quality_programs: Adopted new measures across IQR/VBP/HAC/Readmissions; finalized the Transforming Episode Accountability Model (TEAM) - a mandatory bundled-payment model beginning Jan 1, 2026.

key_changes: NTAP raised to 75% for qualifying sickle-cell-disease gene therapies.
Adopted 2023 CBSA delineations for the wage index.
Finalized the mandatory Transforming Episode Accountability Model (TEAM), effective Jan 1, 2026.

2026.total_payment_impact: approximately +$5.0 billion vs FY2025, including about +$2.0 billion in Medicare DSH uncompensated care payments

provider_payment_factors.wage_index: Discontinued the low-wage-index hospital policy (begun in FY2020); applied a transitional/budget-neutral adjustment for affected hospitals.
provider_payment_factors.dsh_uncompensated_care: Projected increase in Medicare DSH uncompensated care payments of about $2.0 billion.
provider_payment_factors.ntap: Updated New Technology Add-on Payments for qualifying technologies for FY2026.
provider_payment_factors.quality_programs: Advanced electronic clinical quality measures (eCQMs) and digital quality measurement; updates across IQR/VBP/HAC/Readmissions; continued TEAM model implementation.

key_changes: +2.6% operating payment update for IQR + meaningful-EHR hospitals.
Ended the low-wage-index hospital payment policy.
New MS-DRGs 209 (complex aortic arch), 213 (endovascular abdominal aorta w/ iliac branch), 318/359/360 (percutaneous coronary atherectomy).
